jokes.text: No way. Last time, you pulled my pants down, and then you tried to choke me with my shoelace. / False. I did choke you with your shoelace.
jokes.setup: Kevin refuses to participate because Dwight previously attacked him inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact correction that he successfully choked Kevin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight characterization - his casual correction of Kevin's 'false' claim while admitting to assault is absurdly matter-of-fact

jokes.text: Okay, with all due respect to everyone here, i think the most worthy opponent of you is you.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks for someone to attack him for demonstration
jokes.punchline: Jim's diplomatic way of saying Dwight is his own worst enemy
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - politely insulting Dwight while appearing helpful. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: Dwight fighting himself with elaborate commentary
jokes.setup: Dwight demonstrates self-defense by fighting himself
jokes.punchline: The visual of Dwight literally attacking and defending himself with play-by-play
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Dwight absurdity - taking self-defense so seriously he fights himself with genuine intensity

jokes.text: Oh, my god, he's making you look like such a fool. / He really is, but not for long.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam watching Dwight fight himself
jokes.punchline: They're genuinely analyzing Dwight's self-combat as if it's a real fight
jokes.explanation: Jim and Pam's deadpan commentary treats Dwight's self-fight as legitimate competition

jokes.text: I did stand-up comedy once. / You did? / Yeah, i killed. / That sounds like it was hilarious. / It was hilarious.
jokes.setup: Michael claims he did stand-up and killed
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic encouragement that Michael completely misses
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael obliviousness to sarcasm, but well-executed character moment

jokes.text: Michael boasts about getting 'like eight emails a day' when Dwight asks about a specific email from corporate.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks about a specific important email
jokes.punchline: Michael treating eight emails as an impressive, overwhelming amount
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's delusion about his importance. Eight emails being 'a lot' reveals how disconnected he is.

jokes.text: Your feelings journal? You told me to put it in the time capsule. / Did you?
jokes.setup: Michael asks if they have 'the journal'
jokes.punchline: Erin assumes he means his feelings journal that she put in a time capsule
jokes.explanation: Great character detail about Michael having a feelings journal and Erin's literal interpretation

jokes.text: The Wall Street Journal article cuts off at '$1.99 to finish the article' right before revealing bankruptcy rumors
jokes.setup: Everyone desperately wants to know what the ominous corporate news article says
jokes.punchline: The article cuts off at a paywall at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Smart modern comedy about paywalls creating suspense at the worst moment. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: You can all have jobs at schrute farms... As human scarecrows. It doesn't pay much and you can't unionize.
jokes.setup: Dwight offers alternative employment if Dunder Mifflin fails
jokes.punchline: The jobs are as human scarecrows with terrible labor conditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - offering 'help' that's actually terrible, with specific anti-union detail that fits his character

jokes.text: I'll catch you on the flippity-flip. Bye!
jokes.setup: Michael ending a phone call
jokes.punchline: His made-up casual goodbye phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael made-up phrase that sounds like he thinks it's cool slang

jokes.text: No, just poopin'. You know how i be. / But it smells like throw-up in here. / Crazy world, lotta smells.
jokes.setup: Michael clearly threw up but claims he was just using the bathroom normally
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical deflection about the world having lots of smells
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Michael's terrible lie and philosophical-sounding deflection

jokes.text: It is time for the nard-dog to take matters into his own paws.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about taking action regarding Erin
jokes.punchline: Using 'paws' instead of 'hands' to match his 'nard-dog' nickname
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy self-referential nickname humor with wordplay, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Monkey see, monkey do. / That's it. / Monkey pee all over you. / That... Rhymes.
jokes.setup: Jim uses the phrase 'monkey see, monkey do' about following leadership
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude extension of the phrase and self-congratulation for rhyming
jokes.explanation: Basic Michael toilet humor with his pride in making a rhyme, but fairly crude

jokes.text: Michael's stress eating: 'Oh, god! I can't think. I need more mullins.'
jokes.setup: Michael is stressed about the company crisis
jokes.punchline: His solution is to eat more muffins, calling them 'mullins'
jokes.explanation: Michael's stress response and mispronunciation of muffins is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: There has been a murder! / What? / A mur-- / what? / There's been a murder... In savannah.
jokes.setup: Michael dramatically announces a murder during a tense meeting about job security
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be a game set in Savannah, not real
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that builds tension before revealing the game premise. Perfect timing on the reveal.

jokes.text: 'Operation' got me through my vasectomy, i.e. my operation.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how games help with stressful situations
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary clarification that his vasectomy was an operation
jokes.explanation: Mild Michael over-explanation joke, but the wordplay isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: He was gonna call it tube city. So, yes, i do owe him one.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he owes Michael
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of hamsters in office tubes and Jim's resignation to owing Michael
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the callback with Jim's weary acceptance of Michael's logic. Great visual absurdity.

jokes.text: So if you talk slowly in real life, your character could, say, have been kicked in the head by a horse.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how to create character backstories
jokes.punchline: His insensitive suggestion about someone's speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate comment disguised as helpful instruction

jokes.text: 'Nathaniel nutmeg, the local bartender and... Nellie's brother.'
jokes.setup: Andy and Angela discovering they're playing siblings in the game
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable pause and realization after their flirtation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with the incestuous implications of their assigned characters

jokes.text: Every night, a different woman, being oogled, having to hug and kiss and spoon. Oh! I make them feel beautiful.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about his playboy character
jokes.punchline: His reluctant admission that he makes women feel beautiful
jokes.explanation: Michael's fake reluctance about being desired, ending with self-congratulation

jokes.text: It's my blackberry, michael. I'm trying to get updates on the company. / Who's michael? I'm caleb crawdad, i do declare.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to check real news during the game
jokes.punchline: Michael's refusal to break character during a crisis
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's inappropriate priorities and commitment to the game

jokes.text: You don't have to keep saying 'i do declare.' anytime you say something it means you're declaring.
jokes.setup: Michael repeatedly saying 'I do declare' in his Southern accent
jokes.punchline: Oscar's logical explanation of why the phrase is redundant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Oscar line that's both grammatically correct and deflating to Michael's performance

jokes.text: That is the way southern people talk. / And what designing woman are you basing that on? / Delta burke, i do declare.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his Southern accent
jokes.punchline: His specific reference to Delta Burke from Designing Women
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Michael's limited cultural references for Southern accents

jokes.text: Jim coaching Pam's accent: 'You got this kind of like Florida panhandle thing goin', whereas what you really want is more of a Savannah accent, which is more like molasses, just sort of spillin' out of your mouth.'
jokes.setup: Pam is struggling with her Southern accent
jokes.punchline: Jim's overly detailed and pretentious accent coaching
jokes.explanation: Jim's unexpected expertise and flowery description ('molasses spillin' out') is good character work.

jokes.text: Oh, now do the swedish chef. / Uh, i'm not familiar. What province is he from? / He lives on sesame street, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Kevin asking for Swedish Chef impression
jokes.punchline: Oscar's serious question and Kevin calling him dumbass for not knowing a Muppet
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Kevin calling Oscar dumb for not knowing pop culture

jokes.text: No, no, no. Don't turn this into a political thing. / I will poison your food.
jokes.setup: Stanley making class warfare comments as his butler character
jokes.punchline: His immediate escalation to threatening poisoning
jokes.explanation: Great Stanley moment showing his commitment to the game through extreme threats

jokes.text: I would be remiss if i did not ask the pretty young lady out on a date for this weekend. / Sounds like a plan, sugar. / All right! A plan it is.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Angela out while in character
jokes.punchline: His excitement over what he thinks is acceptance
jokes.explanation: Setup for later confusion about whether the date was real or in-character

jokes.text: It's not my fault. I was exposed to harry potter.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his voodoo character's dark arts involvement
jokes.punchline: Blaming Harry Potter exposure for corrupting him
jokes.explanation: Good Stanley deadpan delivery treating Harry Potter as gateway to actual witchcraft

jokes.text: It's never the person you most suspect. It's also never the person you least suspect, since anyone with half a brain would suspect them the most. Therefore, i know the killer to be phyllis, the person i most medium suspect.
jokes.setup: Dwight analyzing who the murderer might be using logic
jokes.punchline: His convoluted reasoning leading to 'most medium suspect'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic that sounds sophisticated but leads to an absurd conclusion. Perfect character integration and quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: Well, you know old nellie is always up for a romp in the hay. / How 'bout a threesome? / Yeah. My boudoir's always open.
jokes.setup: Angela playing a promiscuous character
jokes.punchline: Meredith immediately escalating to inappropriate suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast with Angela playing against type and Meredith being herself

jokes.text: I'm a little worried i might have asked out naughty nellie and not erin. Which would be a whole lot less appealing, because naughty nellie says yes to everyone, and she might be a murderer.
jokes.setup: Andy's confusion about whether his date invitation was real or in-character
jokes.punchline: His reasoning about why dating the character would be worse
jokes.explanation: Good Andy neurosis about the distinction between character and person

jokes.text: This plantation, we're runnin' low on, uh, greenbacks, and we're havin' problems payin' the people who give us the seeds and the dirt.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to explain the real corporate financial crisis
jokes.punchline: Translating modern business terms into plantation-era language
jokes.explanation: Clever way to deliver serious business information through the game format

jokes.text: Well, by now, you figured out that old beatrix bourbon was the killer. / What? / Michael, i was doing so well.
jokes.setup: The game's narrator revealing the solution
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's disappointed reaction to being revealed as the murderer
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the game with Phyllis taking it seriously and being disappointed

jokes.text: Did you mean a real date? / No. / Did you? / Totally not.
jokes.setup: Andy and Angela clarifying whether their date was real or part of the game
jokes.punchline: Both clearly disappointed it wasn't real
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with both characters lying about their disappointment

jokes.text: I thought it was for real... So i was excited, but it was just-- it was part of the game.
jokes.setup: Angela's talking head after the awkward date clarification
jokes.punchline: Her rare admission of vulnerability and disappointment
jokes.explanation: Rare vulnerable moment for Angela showing her softer side

jokes.text: I think michael may have snapped. / Or maybe he's just stuck in character. / Well, which is worse? Snapped or stuck? / Both. They're both worse.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam analyzing Michael's behavior
jokes.punchline: Realizing both possibilities are equally bad
jokes.explanation: Good escalating realization that there's no good explanation for Michael's behavior

jokes.text: I'm the dead body, and these are my brain chunks. / Hey, shut up. You're dead.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his crime scene recreation
jokes.punchline: Immediately telling himself to shut up because he's supposed to be dead
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with Michael correcting himself mid-explanation

jokes.text: Tell him i'm not heyah. / You're not gonna answer the phone? / No, i only answer to detective wallace, 'cause i got a warm body in the other room.
jokes.setup: David Wallace calling during the crisis
jokes.punchline: Michael refusing to take the important call because he's committed to the game
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriate priorities - avoiding his boss during a crisis to maintain game immersion

jokes.text: There has been another murder. / A murder, you say? / I do declare.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing another game murder
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic participation followed by Michael's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Callback to the running gag with Jim's resigned participation

jokes.text: I think today was a good day to have two managers, because if you're a family stuck on a lifeboat in the middle of the ocean, one parent might want to just keep rowing. But if the other parent wants to play a game, it's not because they're crazy. It's because they're doing it for the kids. And i get that now.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on the day's events
jokes.punchline: His elaborate metaphor justifying Michael's inappropriate game during the crisis
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim moment showing growth and understanding of Michael's methods through a perfect metaphor

jokes.text: Today is the hardest i have worked in a long, long time.
jokes.setup: Stanley reflecting on the day spent playing a murder mystery game
jokes.punchline: Calling the game the hardest work he's done
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line that's both self-aware about his work ethic and genuine about his game commitment

jokes.text: Andy revealed himself to be a double agent, at which point, dwight felt comfortable revealing that he also was a double agent, and then, michael announced to everybody that, get this, he was a double agent. Oh, and it is 6:00.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the absurd conclusion of the game
jokes.punchline: Everyone being double agents and the casual mention of quitting time
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with perfect deadpan delivery and timing on the work day ending

jokes.text: Hey, i want to go home. Get the keys out of my purse, start the car.
jokes.setup: The game has devolved into chaos
jokes.punchline: Phyllis treating Bob like a chauffeur to escape the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Phyllis moment showing her taking charge and treating Bob like hired help
